description: Canonical SDS section ordering for document assembly. Consumed by /doc:generate-sds workflow to structure software design specification content. Parallel to fds-structure.json but focused on software architecture and implementation.

based_on.description: SDS is derived from FDS content. This field cross-references the source FDS version for traceability.

available_presets.equipment-first: Primary sections organized by equipment modules (default). Software structure detailed within each module. Matches FDS structure for easy cross-reference.
available_presets.software-first: Primary sections organized by software architecture layers (program organization, FB hierarchy, communication). Equipment modules as implementation details. Alternative for software-heavy projects.

sds_structure.note: This field supports future SDS structure revision without breaking existing projects. Currently only equipment-first is implemented.

children.description: Overall TIA Portal project organization: OBs, FCs, FBs, DBs. CPU allocation if multi-PLC.
High-level FB composition showing how equipment modules relate to each other. Mermaid diagram showing FB call tree.
Network topology, protocols (PROFINET, Ethernet/IP), PLC-to-PLC communication, SCADA links.
Library name, version, platform (TIA Portal V18+), last updated, load mode (external path or imported).
Table listing all typicals used in this project, which equipment modules use them, and key interface summary.
Lists equipment modules that need NEW TYPICAL development. May be empty if all modules matched.
Tags exposed to SCADA, OPC UA server configuration, data refresh rates, alarm routing.
S7 communication, shared memory, handshake protocols for multi-PLC systems.
PROFINET, PROFIBUS, EtherNet/IP device configuration and diagnostics.
MES integration, database connections, third-party equipment protocols.
Navigation hierarchy, screen templates, user roles and permissions.
Which equipment modules map to which HMI faceplates, tag binding conventions.
Alarm priority levels, acknowledgment requirements, alarm text templates.
Consolidated list of all PLC tags, data types, addresses. Auto-generated from equipment modules.
All configurable parameters across equipment modules with default values and ranges.
Complete list of all FB instances with DB numbers and CPU assignments.

equipment_modules.description: One subsection per equipment module from source FDS. Subsection numbering follows 4.x pattern where x is sequential and matches FDS section 4.x for cross-reference.

subsections.description: Which typical(s) matched this equipment module, confidence level, library reference. If unmatched: 'NEW TYPICAL NEEDED' marker.
Which FBs compose this module, hierarchy diagram showing parent-child relationships, calling sequence.
DB instances, instance names following naming conventions, PLC/CPU mapping, memory allocation.
Parameter values derived from FDS requirements, typical defaults, engineering overrides. Cross-references FDS section 4.x.3.
Inputs/outputs, interconnections between FBs within this module, data exchange with other modules. Mermaid flowchart.
Mapping from FDS operating states (section 4.x.2) to FB state variables, transition logic, state synchronization.

mapping_conventions.description: How the /doc:generate-sds workflow maps FDS content to SDS sections

static_sections.system-overview: Maps to section 1 (Introduction) - reuses FDS section 1 with software focus adjustment
static_sections.software-architecture: Maps to section 2 (Software Architecture Overview) - derived from FDS system architecture (section 2) but focused on PLC program structure, not hardware
static_sections.typicals-library: Maps to section 3 (Typicals Library Reference) - generated from CATALOG.json and typical matching results
static_sections.interfaces: Maps to section 5 (Interfaces) - derived from FDS section 5 but detailed at software level (tags, protocols, data structures)
static_sections.hmi-scada: Maps to section 6 (HMI/SCADA Integration) - software implementation of FDS HMI visualization requirements
static_sections.appendices: Maps to section 7 (Appendices) - auto-generated from equipment module SDS sections

dynamic_sections.equipment_modules: Maps to section 4.x subsections from FDS section 4.x. Each FDS equipment module generates one SDS equipment module section with 6 required subsections. Numbering maintained for cross-reference (FDS 4.3 → SDS 4.3).

auto_generated.abbreviations: Section 1.4 auto-generated from FDS abbreviations plus SDS-specific additions (FB names, DB references, protocol acronyms)
auto_generated.signal_list: Section 7.1 auto-generated from all equipment module I/O tables (FDS 4.x.5) with added PLC tag names and DB references
auto_generated.parameter_summary: Section 7.2 auto-generated from all equipment module parameter configuration sections (SDS 4.x.4)
auto_generated.fb_instance_list: Section 7.3 auto-generated from all equipment module instantiation sections (SDS 4.x.3)

assembly_workflow_instructions.step_1: Verify source FDS exists and passes verification (VERIFICATION.md must show PASS)
assembly_workflow_instructions.step_2: Load project-specific typicals library from PROJECT.md typicals.library_path or typicals.imported_path
assembly_workflow_instructions.step_3: Parse FDS ROADMAP.md and fds-structure.json to discover all equipment modules (section 4.x)
assembly_workflow_instructions.step_4: For each equipment module: run typical matching algorithm, generate typical_assignment subsection with confidence and status
assembly_workflow_instructions.step_5: Create SDS section 4.x for each FDS section 4.x with 6 required subsections per dynamic_sections.equipment_modules template
assembly_workflow_instructions.step_6: Derive static sections 1, 2, 5, 6 from corresponding FDS sections with software implementation focus
assembly_workflow_instructions.step_7: Generate section 3 (Typicals Library Reference) from CATALOG.json and matching results, including unmatched equipment list
assembly_workflow_instructions.step_8: For any required section without content, insert placeholder_stub text
assembly_workflow_instructions.step_9: Auto-generate appendices: abbreviations (1.4), signal list (7.1), parameter summary (7.2), FB instance list (7.3)
assembly_workflow_instructions.step_10: Number all sections according to hierarchy, build cross-reference table to FDS sections
assembly_workflow_instructions.step_11: Generate TRACEABILITY.md linking every FDS requirement to SDS implementation section
assembly_workflow_instructions.step_12: Assemble final document with: frontmatter (title page, revision history, ToC, abbreviations) + body (sections 1-7) + appendices
